---
title: '36732516'
author: "Zixuan Lang"
date: "2025-12-14"
output: pdf_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

# Read Data

```{r}
library(dplyr)
library(tm)
library(SnowballC)
library(topicmodels)
library(slam)
library(ggplot2)
library(stats)
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(cluster)
library(factoextra)
library(tidyverse)
library(reshape2)
library(tidytext)
library(ineq)
library(lsa)
library(arules)
library(scales)
```

```{r cars}
# import dataset
setwd("C:\\Users\\lzx\\Desktop")
data<-read.csv("journal_data_CW4.csv")
data<-data.frame(data)
data_selected <- data %>% select(title, journal, abstract, pages, views, doc_number)

```

# Data Preproccess

```{r}
# Create text corpora
title_corpus <- Corpus(VectorSource(data_selected$title))
abstract_corpus <- Corpus(VectorSource(data_selected$abstract))

# Define a common cleaning function
clean_corpus <- function(corpus){
  corpus <- tm_map(corpus, content_transformer(tolower))      # Convert to lowercase
  corpus <- tm_map(corpus, removePunctuation)                 # Remove punctuation
  corpus <- tm_map(corpus, removeNumbers)                     # Remove numbers
  corpus <- tm_map(corpus, removeWords, stopwords("english")) # Remove stopwords
  corpus <- tm_map(corpus, stripWhitespace)                   # Remove extra whitespace
  corpus <- tm_map(corpus, stemDocument)                      # Apply stemming
  return(corpus)
}

# Clean title and abstract corpora
title_corpus_clean <- clean_corpus(title_corpus)
abstract_corpus_clean <- clean_corpus(abstract_corpus)

```

# Bag of Words

```{r}
## =========================
## 1. Convert title / abstract to long format
## =========================
text_data <- data_selected %>%
  select(doc_number, title, abstract) %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = c(title, abstract),
    names_to = "source",
    values_to = "text"
  )

## =========================
## 2. Tokenization + text preprocessing
## =========================
tidy_words <- text_data %>%
  unnest_tokens(word, text) %>%                  # Tokenization + lowercase
  filter(!str_detect(word, "^[0-9]+$")) %>%      # Remove numbers
  filter(!str_detect(word, "^[[:punct:]]+$")) %>%# Remove punctuation
  anti_join(stop_words, by = "word") %>%         # Remove stopwords
  mutate(
    word = wordStem(word, language = "english")  # Apply stemming
  )

## =========================
## 3. Compute TF
## =========================
tf_table <- tidy_words %>%
  count(doc_number, source, word, name = "tf")

## =========================
## 4. Compute IDF + TF-IDF
## =========================
tf_idf_table <- tf_table %>%
  unite(doc_id, doc_number, source, remove = FALSE) %>%
  bind_tf_idf(
    term = word,
    document = doc_id,
    n = tf
  ) %>%
  select(-doc_id)

## =========================
## 5. Merge results (final output)
## =========================
final_word_stats <- tf_idf_table %>%
  arrange(source, doc_number, desc(tf_idf))

title_top_words <- final_word_stats %>%
  filter(source == "title") %>%            # Title only
  group_by(word) %>%                       # Aggregate by word
  summarise(
    total_tf_idf = sum(tf_idf),             # Total TF-IDF across all documents
    total_tf     = sum(tf),                 # Total term frequency
    total_idf    = sum(idf),                # Total IDF
    n_docs       = n_distinct(doc_number)   # Number of documents containing the word
  ) %>%
  arrange(desc(total_tf_idf))               # Sort by total TF-IDF

abstract_top_words <- final_word_stats %>%
  filter(source == "abstract") %>%
  group_by(word) %>%
  summarise(
    total_tf_idf = sum(tf_idf),             # Total TF-IDF across all documents
    total_tf     = sum(tf),                 # Total term frequency
    total_idf    = sum(idf),                # Total IDF
    n_docs       = n_distinct(doc_number)
  ) %>%
  arrange(desc(total_tf_idf))

## =========================
## 6. Example: inspect results
## =========================

title_top_words %>% slice_head(n = 10)
abstract_top_words %>% slice_head(n = 10)

```

# LDA Generate Topic Distribution

```{r}
# Define the number of topics
K_title <- 10        # Number of topics for titles
K_abstract <- 20     # Number of topics for abstracts

# -------------------------------
# Build Document-Term Matrices (input for LDA)
# -------------------------------
dtm_title <- DocumentTermMatrix(
  title_corpus_clean,
  control = list(weighting = weightTf)
)

dtm_abstract <- DocumentTermMatrix(
  abstract_corpus_clean,
  control = list(weighting = weightTf)
)

# -------------------------------
# Train LDA models
# -------------------------------
set.seed(1234)
lda_title <- LDA(dtm_title, k = K_title, control = list(seed = 1234))
lda_abstract <- LDA(dtm_abstract, k = K_abstract, control = list(seed = 1234))



# Retrain LDA models after removing low-frequency components
# to further prune topics and words and reduce input dimensionality

# ================================
# Set thresholds
# ================================
topic_threshold <- 0.03   # Threshold for average topic proportion across documents
word_threshold  <- 1e-4   # Threshold for average word probability across topics
                            # (Dirichlet prior–related filtering)

# ================================
# Title: filter topics and words
# ================================
theta_title <- posterior(lda_title)$topics   # Document–topic distribution
phi_title   <- posterior(lda_title)$terms    # Topic–word distribution

# Filter topics
keep_topics_title <- colMeans(theta_title) >= topic_threshold
K_title_new <- sum(keep_topics_title)

# Filter words
keep_words_title <- colMeans(phi_title) >= word_threshold
dtm_title_reduced <- dtm_title[, keep_words_title]

# Remove empty documents
dtm_title_reduced <- dtm_title_reduced[
  slam::row_sums(dtm_title_reduced) > 0, 
]

# ================================
# Title: retrain LDA
# ================================
set.seed(1234)
lda_title_retrained <- LDA(
  dtm_title_reduced,
  k = K_title_new,
  control = list(seed = 1234)
)

# Extract updated document–topic and topic–word matrices
theta_title_new <- posterior(lda_title_retrained)$topics
phi_title_new   <- posterior(lda_title_retrained)$terms

# ================================
# Abstract: filter topics and words
# ================================
theta_abs <- posterior(lda_abstract)$topics
phi_abs   <- posterior(lda_abstract)$terms

# Filter topics
keep_topics_abs <- colMeans(theta_abs) >= topic_threshold
K_abstract_new <- sum(keep_topics_abs)

# Filter words
keep_words_abs <- colMeans(phi_abs) >= word_threshold
dtm_abstract_reduced <- dtm_abstract[, keep_words_abs]

# Remove empty documents
dtm_abstract_reduced <- dtm_abstract_reduced[
  slam::row_sums(dtm_abstract_reduced) > 0, 
]

# ================================
# Abstract: retrain LDA
# ================================
set.seed(1234)
lda_abstract_retrained <- LDA(
  dtm_abstract_reduced,
  k = K_abstract_new,
  control = list(seed = 1234)
)

# Extract updated document–topic and topic–word matrices
theta_abstract_new <- posterior(lda_abstract_retrained)$topics
phi_abstract_new   <- posterior(lda_abstract_retrained)$terms

```

# LDA Demonstration

```{r}
# ======================================
# LDA result visualization: Topic–Word & Document–Topic
# Dependencies:
# phi_title_new, phi_abstract_new
# theta_title_new, theta_abstract_new
# ======================================

# --------------------------------------
# 1.Prepare φ (Topic–Word) matrix
# --------------------------------------
phi_to_long <- function(phi_matrix, type = "abstract") {
  phi_df <- as.data.frame(phi_matrix)
  phi_df$topic <- factor(seq_len(nrow(phi_df)))

  phi_df %>%
    pivot_longer(
      cols = -topic,
      names_to = "term",
      values_to = "beta"
    ) %>%
    mutate(type = type)
}

phi_abstract_long <- phi_to_long(phi_abstract_new, "abstract")
phi_title_long    <- phi_to_long(phi_title_new, "title")

# --------------------------------------
# 2.Top-N words per topic (by β)
# --------------------------------------
get_top_words <- function(phi_long, top_n = 10) {
  phi_long %>%
    group_by(topic) %>%
    slice_max(order_by = beta, n = top_n, with_ties = FALSE) %>%
    ungroup()
}

top_words_abs   <- get_top_words(phi_abstract_long, top_n = 10)
top_words_title <- get_top_words(phi_title_long, top_n = 10)

# --------------------------------------
# 3.Topic–Word visualization (β bar plots)
# --------------------------------------
plot_topic_words <- function(top_words_df, title_text) {
  ggplot(
    top_words_df,
    aes(
      x = reorder(term, beta),
      y = beta,
      fill = topic
    )
  ) +
    geom_col(show.legend = FALSE) +
    coord_flip() +
    facet_wrap(~ topic, scales = "free") +
    labs(
      title = title_text,
      x = NULL,
      y = expression(beta)
    ) +
    theme_minimal()
}

plot_topic_words(
  top_words_abs,
  "Top words per topic (Abstract, by β)"
)

plot_topic_words(
  top_words_title,
  "Top words per topic (Title, by β)"
)

# --------------------------------------
# 4.Prepare θ (Document–Topic proportion)
# --------------------------------------
theta_to_long <- function(theta_matrix, type = "abstract") {
  theta_df <- as.data.frame(theta_matrix)
  theta_df$document <- seq_len(nrow(theta_df))

  theta_df %>%
    pivot_longer(
      cols = -document,
      names_to = "topic",
      values_to = "proportion"
    ) %>%
    mutate(type = type)
}

theta_abstract_long <- theta_to_long(theta_abstract_new, "abstract")
theta_title_long    <- theta_to_long(theta_title_new, "title")

# --------------------------------------
# 5.Dominant topic per document
# --------------------------------------
get_main_topic <- function(theta_long) {
  theta_long %>%
    group_by(document) %>%
    slice_max(order_by = proportion, n = 1, with_ties = FALSE) %>%
    ungroup()
}

main_topic_abs   <- get_main_topic(theta_abstract_long)
main_topic_title <- get_main_topic(theta_title_long)

# --------------------------------------
# 6.Distribution of dominant topics (Abstract)
# --------------------------------------
ggplot(
  main_topic_abs,
  aes(x = topic, fill = topic)
) +
  geom_bar(show.legend = FALSE) +
  labs(
    title = "Distribution of Dominant Topics (Abstract)",
    x = "Dominant Topic",
    y = "Number of Documents"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()

# --------------------------------------
# 7.Distribution of dominant topics (Title)
# --------------------------------------
ggplot(
  main_topic_title,
  aes(x = topic, fill = topic)
) +
  geom_bar(show.legend = FALSE) +
  labs(
    title = "Distribution of Dominant Topics (Title)",
    x = "Dominant Topic",
    y = "Number of Documents"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()

```

# K-means Cluster and Visualization

```{r}


n <- nrow(data_selected)

# 1.Align rows: pad with zeros
theta_abstract_full <- matrix(
  0,
  nrow = n,
  ncol = ncol(theta_abstract_new)
)
rownames(theta_abstract_full) <- rownames(data_selected)
theta_abstract_full[rownames(theta_abstract_new), ] <- theta_abstract_new

theta_title_full <- matrix(
  0,
  nrow = n,
  ncol = ncol(theta_title_new)
)
rownames(theta_title_full) <- rownames(data_selected)
theta_title_full[rownames(theta_title_new), ] <- theta_title_new

# 2.Merge features
cluster_data <- cbind(theta_title_full, theta_abstract_full)

# standardization for both K-means and PCA
cluster_data_scaled <- scale(cluster_data)

# 3.K-means clustering
set.seed(123)
k <- 5
km_res <- kmeans(cluster_data_scaled, centers = k, nstart = 25)

data_selected$cluster <- factor(km_res$cluster)


# Top-N topics per cluster
top_n_topics_by_cluster <- function(theta_matrix, cluster_labels, n = 5) {
  clusters <- sort(unique(cluster_labels))
  
  result <- lapply(clusters, function(cl) {
    rows <- which(cluster_labels == cl)
    cluster_mean <- colMeans(theta_matrix[rows, , drop = FALSE])
    top_topics <- order(cluster_mean, decreasing = TRUE)[1:n]
    
    data.frame(
      cluster = cl,
      topic_index = top_topics,
      mean_proportion = cluster_mean[top_topics]
    )
  })
  
  do.call(rbind, result)
}

# Compute top topics
top_title_topics <- top_n_topics_by_cluster(
  theta_title_full,
  data_selected$cluster,
  n = 5
)

top_abstract_topics <- top_n_topics_by_cluster(
  theta_abstract_full,
  data_selected$cluster,
  n = 5
)

# 4.PCA dimensionality reduction(for visualization)
pca_res <- prcomp(
  cluster_data_scaled,
  center = TRUE,
  scale. = TRUE
)

# Extract the first two principal components
pca_df <- data.frame(
  PC1 = pca_res$x[, 1],
  PC2 = pca_res$x[, 2],
  cluster = data_selected$cluster
)

# Inspect results
print(top_title_topics)
print(top_abstract_topics)

# 5.PCA 2D visualization
ggplot(pca_df, aes(x = cluster, y = PC1)) +
  geom_boxplot(fill = "lightblue") +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(title = "Distribution of PC1 by Cluster")

ggplot(pca_df, aes(x = cluster, y = PC2)) +
  geom_boxplot(fill = "lightgreen") +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(title = "Distribution of PC2 by Cluster")

```

# Hierarchical Cluster and Visualization

```{r}
n <- nrow(data_selected)

# ===============================
# 1.Align rows: pad with zeros
# ===============================
theta_abstract_full <- matrix(
  0,
  nrow = n,
  ncol = ncol(theta_abstract_new)
)
rownames(theta_abstract_full) <- rownames(data_selected)
theta_abstract_full[rownames(theta_abstract_new), ] <- theta_abstract_new

theta_title_full <- matrix(
  0,
  nrow = n,
  ncol = ncol(theta_title_new)
)
rownames(theta_title_full) <- rownames(data_selected)
theta_title_full[rownames(theta_title_new), ] <- theta_title_new

# ===============================
# 2.Merge features and standardize
# ===============================
cluster_data <- cbind(theta_title_full, theta_abstract_full)
cluster_data_scaled <- scale(cluster_data)

# ===============================
# 3.Hierarchical clustering
# ===============================

# Distance matrix (Euclidean distance, consistent with K-means)
dist_mat <- dist(cluster_data_scaled, method = "euclidean")

# Hierarchical clustering (Ward's method, commonly used and robust)
hc_res <- hclust(dist_mat, method = "ward.D2")

# Plot the dendrogram
plot(
  hc_res,
  labels = FALSE,
  main = "Hierarchical Clustering Dendrogram",
  xlab = "",
  sub = ""
)

# Specify the number of clusters (consistent with k = 5)
k <- 5
hc_clusters <- cutree(hc_res, k = k)

data_selected$cluster <- factor(hc_clusters)

# ===============================
# 4.Top-N topics per cluster
# ===============================

top_n_topics_by_cluster <- function(theta_matrix, cluster_labels, n = 5) {
  clusters <- sort(unique(cluster_labels))
  
  result <- lapply(clusters, function(cl) {
    rows <- which(cluster_labels == cl)
    cluster_mean <- colMeans(theta_matrix[rows, , drop = FALSE])
    top_topics <- order(cluster_mean, decreasing = TRUE)[1:n]
    
    data.frame(
      cluster = cl,
      topic_index = top_topics,
      mean_proportion = cluster_mean[top_topics]
    )
  })
  
  do.call(rbind, result)
}

top_title_topics <- top_n_topics_by_cluster(
  theta_title_full,
  data_selected$cluster,
  n = 5
)

top_abstract_topics <- top_n_topics_by_cluster(
  theta_abstract_full,
  data_selected$cluster,
  n = 5
)

# ===============================
# 5.PCA for visualization only
# ===============================
pca_res <- prcomp(
  cluster_data_scaled,
  center = TRUE,
  scale. = TRUE
)

pca_df <- data.frame(
  PC1 = pca_res$x[, 1],
  PC2 = pca_res$x[, 2],
  cluster = data_selected$cluster
)

# ===============================
# 6.PCA + boxplots
# ===============================
ggplot(pca_df, aes(x = cluster, y = PC1)) +
  geom_boxplot(fill = "lightblue") +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(title = "Distribution of PC1 by Cluster (Hierarchical)")

ggplot(pca_df, aes(x = cluster, y = PC2)) +
  geom_boxplot(fill = "lightgreen") +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(title = "Distribution of PC2 by Cluster (Hierarchical)")

# ===============================
# 7.Inspect results
# ===============================
print(top_title_topics)
print(top_abstract_topics)

```

# Topic Gini Analysis

```{r}

# -------------------------------
# Title: Topic concentration per document (Gini coefficient)
# -------------------------------
theta_title_gini <- apply(theta_title_new, 1, ineq::Gini)

title_gini_df <- data.frame(
  DocID = 1:length(theta_title_gini),
  Gini = theta_title_gini
)

ggplot(title_gini_df, aes(x = DocID, y = Gini)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", fill = "steelblue") +
  labs(
    title = "Document-wise Topic Concentration (Title) - Gini Coefficient",
    x = "Document ID",
    y = "Gini Coefficient"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()

# -------------------------------
# Abstract: Topic concentration per document (Gini coefficient)
# -------------------------------
theta_abs_gini <- apply(theta_abstract_new, 1, ineq::Gini)

abs_gini_df <- data.frame(
  DocID = 1:length(theta_abs_gini),
  Gini = theta_abs_gini
)

ggplot(abs_gini_df, aes(x = DocID, y = Gini)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", fill = "darkorange") +
  labs(
    title = "Document-wise Topic Concentration (Abstract) - Gini Coefficient",
    x = "Document ID",
    y = "Gini Coefficient"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()

```

# Topic one-hot coding

```{r}
# -------------------------------
# Helper: Find dominant topics
# -------------------------------
get_dominant_topics <- function(theta, threshold = 0.5) {
  lapply(1:nrow(theta), function(i) {
    p <- theta[i, ]
    o <- order(p, decreasing = TRUE)
    cum_sum <- cumsum(p[o])
    keep <- o[cum_sum <= threshold]
    if (length(keep) == 0) keep <- o[1]
    return(keep)
  })
}

# -------------------------------
# 1. Dominant topics for Title
# -------------------------------
title_dom <- get_dominant_topics(theta_title_new, threshold = 0.5)

# -------------------------------
# 2. Dominant topics for Abstract
# -------------------------------
abs_dom <- get_dominant_topics(theta_abstract_new, threshold = 0.5)

# -------------------------------
# 3. Combine dominant topics from Title + Abstract
# -------------------------------
combined_dom <- mapply(
  function(t, a) sort(unique(c(t, a))),
  title_dom, abs_dom,
  SIMPLIFY = FALSE
)

# -------------------------------
# 4. Build overall one-hot encoding
# -------------------------------
# Number of possible topics
num_title <- ncol(theta_title_new)
num_abs   <- ncol(theta_abstract_new)

# Create unique column names: Title_1..Title_K, Abstract_1..Abstract_K
all_topic_names <- c(
  paste0("Title_", 1:num_title),
  paste0("Abstract_", 1:num_abs)
)

# Initialize one-hot matrix
onehot_mat <- matrix(
  0,
  nrow = length(combined_dom),
  ncol = length(all_topic_names)
)
colnames(onehot_mat) <- all_topic_names

# Fill one-hot matrix
for (i in seq_along(combined_dom)) {
  idx <- combined_dom[[i]]
  # Separate title and abstract indices
  title_idx <- idx[idx <= num_title]
  abs_idx   <- idx[idx > num_title] - num_title
  if (length(title_idx) > 0) onehot_mat[i, paste0("Title_", title_idx)] <- 1
  if (length(abs_idx) > 0)   onehot_mat[i, paste0("Abstract_", abs_idx)] <- 1
}

onehot_df <- as.data.frame(onehot_mat)

# Merge with original data
data_features <- cbind(data_selected, onehot_df)

# Check invalid columns (example)
which(data_features$Abstract_11==1) 
which(data_features$Abstract_12==1)
which(data_features$Abstract_13==1)
which(data_features$Abstract_14==1) 
which(data_features$Abstract_15==1) 
which(data_features$Abstract_16==1)
which(data_features$Abstract_17==1)
which(data_features$Abstract_18==1)
which(data_features$Abstract_19==1)
which(data_features$Abstract_20==1) 

# Remove all-zero columns and merge with original data
onehot_df_reduced <- onehot_df[, colSums(onehot_df) > 0]
data_features_reduced <- cbind(data_selected, onehot_df_reduced)

```

# Views and pages bins generation

```{r}

# -------------------------------
# 1. Clean and convert views / pages
# -------------------------------
data_features_reduced$views <- gsub(",", "", trimws(data_features_reduced$views))
data_features_reduced$views <- as.numeric(data_features_reduced$views)

data_features_reduced$pages <- gsub(",", "", trimws(data_features_reduced$pages))
data_features_reduced$pages <- as.numeric(data_features_reduced$pages)

# -------------------------------
# 2. Set the number of bins
# -------------------------------
num_bins <- 5  # Can be adjusted as needed

# -------------------------------
# 3. Perform equal-width binning for views and pages
# -------------------------------
data_features_reduced$views_bin <- cut(
  data_features_reduced$views,
  breaks = num_bins,
  include.lowest = TRUE,
  labels = paste0("bin_", 1:num_bins)
)

data_features_reduced$pages_bin <- cut(
  data_features_reduced$pages,
  breaks = num_bins,
  include.lowest = TRUE,
  labels = paste0("bin_", 1:num_bins)
)

```

# Association and Interpretation

```{r}

# -------------------------------
# 1. Identify Title / Abstract topic columns
# -------------------------------
title_cols <- grep("^Title_\\d+$", names(data_features_reduced), value = TRUE)
abstract_cols <- grep("^Abstract_\\d+$", names(data_features_reduced), value = TRUE)

# -------------------------------
# 2. Build transaction list
# -------------------------------
transactions_list <- lapply(1:nrow(data_features_reduced), function(i) {
  c(
    # Title topics
    names(data_features_reduced[i, title_cols])[data_features_reduced[i, title_cols] == 1],
    # Abstract topics
    names(data_features_reduced[i, abstract_cols])[data_features_reduced[i, abstract_cols] == 1],
    # pages_bin
    paste0("pages=", data_features_reduced$pages_bin[i]),
    # views_bin
    paste0("views=", data_features_reduced$views_bin[i])
  )
})

# -------------------------------
# 3. Convert to transactions object
# -------------------------------
trans_all <- as(transactions_list, "transactions")

# -------------------------------
# 4. Define high-traffic views bins
# -------------------------------
high_views_bins <- paste0("views=", c("bin_4", "bin_5"))

# -------------------------------
# 5. Apply Apriori algorithm to mine high-traffic rules
# -------------------------------
rules <- apriori(
  trans_all,
  parameter = list(supp = 0.0001, conf = 0.02, minlen = 1),
  appearance = list(rhs = high_views_bins, default = "lhs")
)

# -------------------------------
# 6. Export rule metrics
# -------------------------------
rules_df <- as(rules, "data.frame")
rules_df <- rules_df[, c("rules", "support", "confidence", "lift")]

# -------------------------------
# 7. Inspect top rules
# -------------------------------
head(rules_df)

```

# Skewed Dataset Demonstration

```{r}
data_selected_show <- data_selected %>% 
  select(views, doc_number) %>%
  # Remove spaces and commas, convert to numeric
  mutate(views = as.numeric(gsub("[ ,]", "", views))) %>%
  arrange(desc(views))  # Sort by views in descending order

# Plot bar chart
ggplot(data_selected_show, aes(x = reorder(doc_number, -views), y = views)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", fill = "steelblue") +
  labs(
    x = "Document Number",
    y = "Views",
    title = "Article Views by Document Number"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))
```
